title: Minimize the admission of containers wishing to share the host IPC namespace

input_row.title: Minimize the admission of containers wishing to share the host IPC namespace
input_row.description: Do not generally permit containers to be run with the hostIPC flag set to true.
input_row.rationale: A container running in the host's IPC namespace can use IPC to interact with processes outside the container. There should be at least one PodSecurityPolicy (PSP) defined which does not permit containers to share the host IPC namespace. If you have a requirement to containers which require hostIPC, this should be defined in a separate PSP and you should carefully check RBAC controls to ensure that only limited service accounts and users are given permission to access that PSP.
input_row.audit: Get the set of PSPs with the following command: kubectl get psp For each PSP, check whether privileged is enabled: kubectl get psp <name> -o=jsonpath='{.spec.hostIPC}' Verify that there is at least one PSP which does not return true.
input_row.remediation: Create a PSP as described in the Kubernetes documentation, ensuring that the .spec.hostIPC field is omitted or set to false.
input_row.impact: Pods defined with spec.hostIPC: true will not be permitted unless they are run under a specific PSP.
input_row.default_value: By default, PodSecurityPolicies are not defined.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_pod_security_hostipc_restrictions

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the command `kubectl get psp` to list all PodSecurityPolicies (PSPs).
- For each PSP, use the command `kubectl get psp <name> -o=jsonpath='{.spec.hostIPC}'` to check the value of the `hostIPC` field.
- The automated check will verify that there is at least one PSP where the `hostIPC` field is either omitted or set to false.
- **Pass Criteria:** At least one PSP must have `hostIPC` set to false or not defined.
- **Fail Criteria:** All PSPs have `hostIPC` set to true.
